mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase borrowing costs and discount rates, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show positive mean in >65% of rising rate periods, or Rank IC < 0.02 over 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and weakening consumer spending, triggering immediate negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in >70% of triggered instances despite the unemployment spike.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real GDP and retail sales indicates synchronized domestic demand growth, which drives corporate earnings upward and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in GDPC1 and RSAFS yields negative 60-day forward returns in >60% of cases, or interaction term Rank IC < 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: An accelerating industrial production index reflects expanding manufacturing capacity and supply-side momentum, which supports higher equity valuations and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rising INDPRO coincides with negative 20-day forward returns in >65% of observations during recovery phases.

mechanism_narrative: High policy rates combined with rising unemployment create stagflationary pressure that compresses profit margins and discount rates, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint condition of high rates and rising unemployment yields positive 20-day returns in >60% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp month-over-month drop in housing starts signals collapsing construction demand and broader economic weakness, triggering immediate negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in >70% of triggered instances despite the housing starts decline.

mechanism_narrative: Steady growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects sustained labor market strength and rising household income, which supports consumer spending and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Rising PAYEMS yields negative 60-day forward returns in >65% of observations, or Rank IC < 0.02 over rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Elevated consumer price inflation forces the Federal Reserve to maintain restrictive policy, which increases equity discount rates and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: High inflation periods show positive 20-day forward returns in >65% of cases, or signal loses predictive power when CPIAUCSL exceeds 3.0%.
